item.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

item.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

item.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

item.title: Tesla spending skyrockets as Cybercab, Semi, Megapack production timeline slips
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane
item.description: Tesla's 26% boost in revenue wasn't enough to offset rising operating expenses and capital expenditures as it pushes to launch a new generation of products.

item.title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A closely watched social media addiction lawsuit that had been set to go to trial next week has been dropped after the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed his claims against Meta, leaving none of the major tech companies facing trial in the case.

item.title: SoundCloud acquires decentralized music platform Nina Protocol months after its shutdown
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: SoundCloud has acquired decentralized music platform Nina Protocol, months after the startup announced it would shut down. The deal brings Nina’s artists, editorial archive, and music discovery tools to SoundCloud as the company continues expanding its platform for independent musicians.

item.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

item.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

item.title: Science Corporation’s vision-restoring chip wins EU approval
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: "The thing that the space needs is a company making $100 million a year of revenue," Science Corp. CEO Max Hodak said.

item.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by messaging, photo sharing, and AI features designed to strengthen real-world relationships.

item.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains why Anthropic is winning (and it’s not the model)
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not in the internet wave, not in mobile, not in the first cloud boom. Menlo led Anthropic’s $500M Series D, and Murphy has had a front-row seat as the company went from a pre-revenue, […]

item.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The company said it is reducing its headcount by 20%, or about 630 staff, to "support a leaner, more focused operating model" as it focuses on its AI Work Platform.

item.title: Google is making it easier to switch from iPhone to Android
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The new feature lets users wirelessly transfer more data types from an iPhone without needing to download a separate app.

item.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

item.title: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Substack is giving readers a way to estimate how much of a newsletter was written by AI, signaling a broader shift toward transparency around AI-assisted content.

item.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.

item.title: If you pay a hacker’s ransom, chances are that they’ll come back for more
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The long-held understanding among security researchers and network defenders is that it's impossible to negotiate in good faith with an extortion racket because there's no incentive for the other side to actually walk away.

item.title: Cascade raises $3.5M to help construction firms find and win projects
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: a16z Speedrun, Ada Ventures, and Snowball VC have invested in Cascade's $3.5 million seed round.

item.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Passionfroot, a German startup building a marketplace connecting B2B creators with brands, has raised $15M in a Series A round led by Insight Partners.
